_comment: Deterministic CodeQL expectations. 'expected' findings MUST be present; 'forbidden' findings MUST be absent. Matching is by ruleId plus a substring of the SARIF result file path. See scripts/check_findings.py.

expected.why: Control: load(query) with no sanitizer must be flagged. Proves the sink + flow work, so the matching treatment's silence is attributable to the barrier.

forbidden.why: Treatment: identical to the control except for resolvedWithinRoot(). The barrier model must suppress the path-injection finding. Any alert here means the barrier failed.
http4k strips CR/LF from header names/values internally (sanitizeHeader -> withoutCrLf), so response splitting via Response.header is not exploitable. The sink was removed; no alert should appear.
Same framework-internal CR/LF sanitization applies to the redirect endpoints.
